sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.12 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Given a finite$p$-group$G$, we define a$$-extension\/ of$G$as any finite$p$-group$H$containing a normal subgroup$N$of order$p$such that$H/N G$and$N (H)$. Is it true that for every finite$p$-group$G$there exists an infinite sequence$G=G_1,\,G_2,$such that$G_{i+1}$is a$$-extension of$G_i$for all$i=1,\,2,$? { Ya.\,G.\,Berkovich} Yes, it is (S.\,F.\,Kamornikov, Izv. Gomel' Univ., 5 (2008), 200--201 (R...
